summary: Frontend-only iter37 testing for Siri/Google Assistant voice-shortcut feature. All 13 requested cases passed: /play deep-link route (preset, custom freq, alias, BOGUS, no-params, public access, Open-app fallback), VoiceShortcutsModal (button, tabs, 6 preset cards, copy-to-clipboard with 'Copied' state + reset, X close), and Dashboard header regression (all 6 buttons present).
critical_code_review_comments: PlayDeepLink.jsx useCallback for applyConfig/startPlayback has empty deps [] but references cfg object built each render — current behaviour works because applyConfig only runs from the mount effect, but is technically a stale-closure smell. Low priority.
PlayDeepLink unmount comment notes 'do not auto-stop' to keep audio alive — confirmed working via Open-full-app handoff. Good UX.
VoiceShortcutsModal copy() silently swallows clipboard errors (just console.warn). Consider surfacing a toast on failure (e.g., HTTP context without clipboard permission). Low priority.
Dashboard.jsx file is large (>1500 lines based on grep). Consider splitting header into a separate component to ease future maintenance. Pre-existing, not iter37-introduced.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter37 voice-shortcuts feature fully verified. /play route is public (works without auth as required). VoiceShortcutsModal copy button uses navigator.clipboard.writeText and reverts to 'Copy URL' after exactly ~1.5s. Aliases 'relax'→Calm and 'deep sleep'→Sleep both work via resolvePreset. Custom frequency URL (no preset) renders '432 Hz' title and no error. /play?preset=BOGUS correctly falls through to deeplink-error. Headless Chromium reports status='Ready' (autoplay blocked) — expected; needsGesture branch works. The 401 console errors on /play are expected (the engine/page tries /api/auth/me without cookie — harmless on public route).
